source_name: European Banking Authority
title: ​The EBA publishes final Guidelines on the authorisation of third-country branches under the Capital Requirements Directive
clean_title: ​The EBA publishes final Guidelines on the authorisation of third-country branches under the Capital Requirements Directive
summary: ​The European Banking Authority (EBA) today published its final Guidelines on authorisation of third-country credit institutions to set-up a third-country branch (TCB) in a Member State. These Guidelines were mandated by the Capital Requirements Directive (CRD6) which introduced a new regime for TCBs and contribute to the harmonised access to the EU market for banking services.
why_selected: Thématique finance ; source jugée fiable (european banking authority) ; impact réglementaire.
impacts: Peut modifier la conformité, les reportings ou la gouvernance des acteurs financiers.
title_signature: authorisation branches capital country directive eba final guidelines publishes requirements
normalized_title: the eba publishes final guidelines on the authorisation of third country branches under the capital requirements directive

title: The ESAs support ESRB warning on systemic cyber risks from frontier AI models
